w:r.w:t: VR: Presentation (Either Philip/ Nick can hopefully present)
Provided by Philip or Nick ideally)
Ai may not be used, except in the dedicated research time or when building own AI for project
Moderators will walk around to ensure groups are not cheating (All willing CLC members)
may be used in the project
The best 3 projects will all receive
will be determined later what prizes)
The judges favourite project will receive an additional prize (Whether it be tools implemented or certain pieces of code)
The project that uses the most “out- of syllabus” tools will get a prize
one of the following topics to attempts
Sensors should detect how someone is moving around in a house and only switch on the lights in the room with people and 1 surrounding room (if students want to do
they can create a menu to ask how many adjacent rooms should light up to the customer)
A sensor should detect when a user approaches a door and
3. Fingerprints (as the touch the door handle)
Students should build a system to help customers find parking, this could include a system where each parking bay has a sensor and when the users enter the parking lot an LED pathway
appears in the road guiding them to the designated parking (a system could be used that when a customer steals a parking spot for some reason, a new route should appear)
